meta.name: Stovetop Naan-Style Bread
meta.subtitle: Soft • blistered • butter-brushed • great with curries & stews • fresh milled or commercial flour

source.notes: A stovetop naan-style flatbread using yogurt + optional yeast for flavor. Designed for skillet or cast-iron cooking with classic blistering and soft interior.

tasteProfile.notes: Tangy yogurt crumb, lightly sweetened dough, blistered exterior, butter/garlic finish.

ingredients.label: Flour (all-purpose or bread flour; or fresh milled wheat flour—see Fresh-Milled Notes)
ingredients.notes: All-purpose yields classic soft naan. Bread flour gives a slightly chewier bite.

ingredients.label: Sugar
ingredients.notes: About 1 tablespoon. Helps browning and balances yogurt tang.

ingredients.label: Instant yeast (recommended) or active dry yeast
ingredients.notes: About 1 1/4 teaspoons. If using active dry, bloom in warm water first (see steps).

ingredients.label: Baking powder
ingredients.notes: About 1 1/2 teaspoons. Adds extra puff for stovetop cooking.

ingredients.label: Plain yogurt (whole milk preferred)
ingredients.notes: Greek yogurt works; thin with a little extra water if very thick.

ingredients.label: Warm water (about 95–105°F / 35–40°C)
ingredients.notes: Hold back 20–30g and add only if dough feels dry.

ingredients.label: Neutral oil or melted ghee (in dough)
Oil for pan (as needed)
ingredients.notes: Usually a very light film; cast iron may need less.

steps.title: Optional: bloom active dry yeast (if not using instant)
instructions: If using active dry yeast, stir yeast and sugar into the warm water (use water from recipe total).
Rest 5–10 minutes until foamy. If using instant yeast, skip this step.

steps.title: Mix dry ingredients
instructions: In a bowl, whisk flour, salt, baking powder, and (if using instant yeast) the yeast.

steps.title: Add wet ingredients and form dough
instructions: Add yogurt, oil/ghee, and most of the warm water (or bloomed yeast-water). Hold back ~20–30g water.
Mix until a shaggy dough forms. Knead 6–8 minutes by hand (or 4–5 minutes in mixer on low) until smooth and elastic.
Add reserved water only if dough is dry and cracking. Dough should be soft and slightly tacky, not wet.

steps.title: First rise (bulk)
instructions: Place dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover, and rise until puffy and about doubled, 60–75 minutes.

steps.title: Divide and rest
instructions: Turn dough out and divide into 6 equal pieces (~135g each).
Round into balls and cover. Rest 10 minutes so the dough relaxes for rolling.

steps.title: Shape naan (teardrops or ovals)
instructions: On a lightly floured surface, roll each ball into an oval/teardrop about 1/8–3/16 inch (3–5 mm) thick.
For classic naan texture, keep them slightly thicker than a tortilla.

steps.title: Cook on hot skillet (blisters + puff)
instructions: Heat a skillet (cast iron preferred) over medium-high until very hot. Lightly oil the pan.
Place one naan in the skillet. Cook 60–90 seconds until bubbles form and the underside has deep brown spots.
Flip and cook 45–75 seconds more. For extra puff, cover the skillet briefly after flipping to trap steam.
Optional: for deeper char, briefly press thicker areas with a spatula or cook 10–15 seconds longer per side.

steps.title: Brush and finish (butter/garlic/herbs)
instructions: Immediately brush hot naan with melted butter/ghee.
For garlic naan: stir minced garlic into melted butter and brush; sprinkle with cilantro if desired.
Stack naan in a towel to keep soft.

freshMilledNotes.summary: Fresh milled flour can be thirstier and less extensible. Blending and resting improves tenderness and roll-out.
guidelines: Best everyday naan texture: use 70% fresh milled flour + 30% all-purpose flour (same total flour weight).
If using 100% fresh milled (unsifted), increase water by 15–35g as needed and extend the bench rest to 15–20 minutes.
If dough tears while rolling, rest 10 minutes and roll again; fresh milled dough often needs extra relaxation time.
Sifting/bolting fresh milled flour removes some bran and produces softer naan.

variations.name: No-yeast quick naan (still very good)
variations.change: Omit yeast. Increase baking powder to 10g. Rest dough 15–20 minutes instead of rising 60–75 minutes.
variations.notes: Less developed flavor but fast and soft.

variations.name: Whole wheat naan (70/30 blend)
variations.change: Use 70% whole wheat flour + 30% all-purpose. Add 15–25g extra water if needed.
variations.notes: Softer than 100% whole wheat, still hearty.

variations.name: Ghee-rich naan
variations.change: Replace oil in dough with melted ghee (20g) and brush with ghee after cooking.
variations.notes: Very aromatic, classic pairing for curries.

variations.name: Nigella / sesame naan
variations.change: After shaping, lightly brush one side with water and sprinkle nigella or sesame; cook seed-side down first.

storage.counter: Best same day. Keep wrapped in a towel then airtight up to 1 day.
storage.fridge: Up to 4 days airtight. Rewarm in a dry skillet 20–40 seconds per side or microwave briefly under a damp towel.
storage.freezer: Freeze stacked with parchment between naan up to 2 months. Rewarm from frozen in a skillet or toaster oven.

notes: Key for good naan on the stove: very hot pan + slightly thicker roll + steam trap (lid) after flipping.
If naan browns too quickly before puffing, reduce heat slightly. If pale and dry, increase heat.
Stacking in a towel keeps naan soft; leaving them uncovered will dry them quickly.

audit.testNotes: Stovetop naan-style recipe optimized for soft texture and blistering. Includes instant/active dry yeast handling and a no-yeast speed option.
